DISCOVERY 301,PETITIONER DEUTSCHE BANK SECURTIES INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEBTOR TO EXERCISE STOCK OPTIONS AND TURN OVER SHARES PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA COMMERICAL CODE 8112 (E) - THE COURT CONFIRMED THE TENTATIVE RULING EXCEPT THE EXCERCISE OF OPTION IS ALSO POTENTIALLY SUBJECT TO SECURITY INTEREST. A) THAT PLAINTIFF GIVE DEFENDANT THE EXERCISE PRICE FOR THE OPTIONS AND THAT DEFENDANT EXERCISE THEM AND TURN THE SHARES OVER TO PLAINTIFF, SUBJECT TO ANY SENIOR INTERESTS THAT MAY EXIST IN THOSE SHARES, AND OR B) THAT DEFENDANT NOT FURTHER ENCUMBER HIS OPTIONS OR THE SHARES TO WHICH THEY ENTITLE THEM. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER AND MAY INCLUDE THAT JUDGMENT DEBTOR WILL NOT DO ANYTHING TO ENCUMBER THE SHARE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice And Motion To Compel Debtor To Exercise Stock Options And Turn Over Shares Pursuant To California Commerical Code 8112 (E)
Off Calendar Feb-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHAN PEROVICH OF THE LAW OFFICES OF KEESAL, YOUNG & LOGAN, TEL. NO. (562) 436-2000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN CHU, TEL. NO. 989-5300 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT AND JUDGMENT DEBTOR JOHN BANTLEMAN.

RULING - A R G U E D; PETITIONER DEUTSCHE BANK SECURTIES INCORPORATED'S Motion To Compel Debtor To Exercise Stock Options And Turn Over Shares Pursuant To California Commerical Code 8112 (e) - THE COURT CONFIRMED THE TENTATIVE RULING EXCEPT THE EXCERCISE OF OPTION IS ALSO POTENTIALLY SUBJECT TO SECURITY INTEREST. a) that Plaintiff give Defendant the exercise price for the options and that Defendant exercise them and turn the shares over to Plaintiff, subject to any senior interests that may exist in those shares, and or b) that Defendant not further encumber his options or the shares to which they entitle them. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER AND MAY INCLUDE THAT JUDGMENT DEBTOR WILL NOT DO ANYTHING TO ENCUMBER THE SHARE. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported. = (301/PJB)
